parts.answer: R1 1 R1 & R2 1 + 1 R1 & R2 R3 & R4 TOTAL 20 when he visited New York City (paragraph 1, ‘Of my wanderings «’). • problem of doing jobs in the rain/many jobs • he had to do them all in that day/he was leaving New York that night/pressure of time (paragraph 1, ‘Of my wanderings «’). • he (frequently) had to find shelter/go inside • it rained so hard that his raincoat was becoming: wet on the inside/he got very wet/drenched/soaked. NB: don’t accept ‘clothes’ or ‘shoes’ getting wet • he left a puddle on the floor/dripped all over the floor of every building/all the places he entered attempts at evasion’ (lines 10–11). • ignoring/discounting my efforts to avoid the question/not allowing him to avoid the question • he (the writer) tried/attempted to avoid answering the question October/November Question Answer Marks behaved towards the writer and how the writer reacted to this behaviour (paragraph 3, ‘Similarly, in a «’). • the Manager treated the writer with suspicion/thought he was dishonest • he refused to give the writer any assistance/help him look up books/was rude/unkind • the writer lost his temper/told him he was a stranger and didn’t expect this kind of treatment/said it wouldn’t happen in England towards the writer (paragraph 3, ‘Similarly, in a «’). • examples accepted: he gave addresses (of those who could help)/came out in the rain/directed him to a restaurant • he went out of his way to help/became very helpful Accept a relevant selective quote but not a lifted chunk. What the writer did with his wet clothes when he returned to the hotel (paragraph 4, ‘I was so wet «’)? • he left his (wet) clothes (shoes, socks, trousers) behind/he did not pack them/threw them on kitchen floor NB: do not credit ‘he took his clothes off’. • there wasn’t time to dry them out/they were too wet to pack/they would have made all the rest of his possessions wet if he had packed them as the writer uses them. Then explain how the phrases help to suggest the narrator’s thoughts about
